seo.title: Building Workflow Automation with DSPy + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build workflow automation with DSPy + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: DSPy enables programming LLMs as composable modules for workflow automation, orchestrating multi-step agents with API calls, conditionals, and human approvals. Guardrails are essential to prevent costly errors in production workflows like failed API transactions or unapproved actions. Vex provides runtime verification to catch DSPy failure modes before they cascade through your automation pipeline.

architectureOverview.heading: Workflow Automation Architecture with DSPy
architectureOverview.description:
A typical DSPy workflow automation agent decomposes tasks into optimized modules: a Planner generates step-by-step execution plans, Router modules handle conditional branching, Action modules execute API calls, and Validator modules check outputs against assertions. Human-in-the-loop approvals are integrated via custom signatures that pause execution for review. The entire pipeline is compiled and optimized against training examples for reliability.

DSPy's teleprompters optimize module prompts automatically, but this can lead to overfitting. Vex verification wraps critical forward() calls to catch assertion failures and unexpected output distributions before they trigger API calls or approvals. Rollback mechanisms use idempotency keys tracked across module calls.

The architecture scales by composing modules into signatures, with Vex providing observability into failure modes across the entire workflow.

risks.name: Optimized prompts overfitting
risks.description: DSPy teleprompters create prompts too specific to training data, failing on real workflow variations
risks.mitigation: Vex verifies output distributions against expected patterns regardless of prompt optimization

risks.name: CoT reasoning logic errors
risks.description: Chain-of-thought modules produce plausible but incorrect execution plans for API sequences
risks.mitigation: Vex action correctness checks validate plan semantics before execution

risks.name: Uncaught assertion failures
risks.description: DSPy modules skip internal assertions during inference, allowing invalid states to propagate
risks.mitigation: vex.verify() wraps forward() calls with runtime assertion enforcement

risks.name: Module composition drift
risks.description: Unexpected output distributions emerge when composing optimized modules in production workflows
risks.mitigation: Vex monitors output schemas and statistical properties across module boundaries

implementation.title: DSPy Workflow Planner Module
implementation.description: Basic DSPy signature for generating multi-step workflow plans with API actions
implementation.code:
import dspy

class WorkflowPlanner(dspy.Signature):
    '''Generate execution plan for workflow automation.'''
    task_description: str = dspy.InputField()
    execution_plan: str = dspy.OutputField(desc='JSON list of steps with API calls')

planner = dspy.ChainOfThought(WorkflowPlanner)

# Usage
plan = planner(task_description='Process customer order #12345').execution_plan
print(plan)

implementation.title: API Action Module with Idempotency
implementation.description: DSPy module for safe API execution with idempotency keys and rollback tracking
implementation.code:
class APIAction(dspy.Signature):
    '''Execute API call safely with idempotency.'''
    api_endpoint: str = dspy.InputField()
    payload: str = dspy.InputField()
    idempotency_key: str = dspy.InputField()
    response: str = dspy.OutputField(desc='JSON response or error')
    rollback_needed: bool = dspy.OutputField(desc='True if rollback required')

action_module = APIAction()
result = action_module(
    api_endpoint='https://api.example.com/orders',
    payload='{"order_id": "12345"}',
    idempotency_key='order-12345-v1'
)

implementation.title: Conditional Router Module
implementation.description: DSPy module for workflow branching based on API response analysis
implementation.code:
class WorkflowRouter(dspy.Signature):
    '''Route workflow based on previous step result.'''
    previous_result: str = dspy.InputField()
    api_response: str = dspy.InputField()
    next_step: str = dspy.OutputField(desc='Next module to execute')
    approval_needed: bool = dspy.OutputField()

router = dspy.ChainOfThought(WorkflowRouter)
route = router(
    previous_result='Order created',
    api_response='{"status": "pending_approval"}'
).next_step

implementation.title: Human Approval Gate
implementation.description: DSPy module that generates approval requests for human-in-the-loop validation
implementation.code:
class ApprovalGate(dspy.Signature):
    '''Generate human approval request.'''
    workflow_context: str = dspy.InputField()
    risky_action: str = dspy.InputField()
    approval_justification: str = dspy.OutputField()
    approval_decision: str = dspy.OutputField(desc='approve|reject|escalate')

approval = ApprovalGate()
result = approval(
    workflow_context='Order #12345: $5000',
    risky_action='Charge credit card'
)

vexIntegration.title: Verify Planner with Action Correctness
vexIntegration.description: Wrap DSPy planner forward() call with Vex to validate execution plans contain valid API patterns
vexIntegration.code:
import vex

@vex.verify(
    assertions=[
        vex.assert_matches('.*https://api\\.example\\.com.*', 'api_endpoint'),
        vex.assert_contains('idempotency_key'),
        vex.assert_not_contains('DELETE', 'method')
    ],
    risk_profile='medium'
)
def safe_plan(task_description: str) -> str:
    planner = dspy.ChainOfThought(WorkflowPlanner())
    result = planner(task_description=task_description)
    return result.execution_plan

# Usage
plan = safe_plan('Process high-value order #12345')

vexIntegration.title: API Action Permission + Idempotency Check
vexIntegration.description: Vex verifies API actions have proper permissions and idempotency before execution

vexIntegration.title: Workflow Router with Rollback Safety
vexIntegration.description: Verify routing decisions don't create unrecoverable states without rollback paths

cta.heading: Secure Your DSPy Workflow Automation
cta.description: Add Vex verification to your DSPy modules today with pip install vex-sdk to prevent production failures.
